mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending that directly boosts corporate earnings and drives equity prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days. A decline in RSAFS growth weakens earnings expectations and leads to negative returns.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0% for two consecutive months, it signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a tighter monetary policy stance, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0 for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If more than 50% of triggered instances produce positive 60-day forward SPX returns, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a coordinated contraction in both supply and demand, which erodes corporate profitability and causes negative equity returns over a 20-day horizon. The joint signal is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 40% of months when both series decline, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over a three-month period signals an accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and elevates equity valuations. In the current easing cycle, this leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism flips to negative in tightening regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month moving average declines by more than 25 bps over the prior month
failure_condition: If the positive return rate in triggered instances during actual Fed easing cycles is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in industrial production (INDPRO) provides a real-time signal of manufacturing strength that directly impacts market sentiment and equity prices over the next week. A rising INDPRO indicates economic vitality and supports positive returns, while a decline signals weakness and leads to negative returns.
failure_condition: If the weekly rank correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation rises above 2.5%, it forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a restrictive monetary policy to curb price pressures, tightening financial conditions and reducing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon. The trigger acts as an early warning for policy tightening.
failure_condition: If forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of the months when CPI exceeds 2.5% YoY, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is low (UNRATE below 4%) and the Fed is actively cutting rates (FEDFUNDS declining by at least 25 bps over three months), it signals a pre-emptive easing to support a strong but slowing economy. This combination provides a positive impulse to equity markets over the next 20 trading days as rate cuts boost valuations and low unemployment supports consumption.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term is not statistically significant in a linear regression (p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of economic momentum. In an expansionary regime (no recession in the prior 12 months), an increase in housing starts signals continued demand growth and supports higher equity prices over the next quarter. A decline in housing starts signals a slowdown and leads to negative forward returns.
regime_hint: expansion phase (no recession in prior 12 months)
failure_condition: If in non-recession periods the rank correlation between HOUST growth and 60-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.03 over any 5-year window, reject.
